set.body:
Objective

Make natural-features the authoritative, lossless temporal-feature bookkeeping layer. It must preserve native sampling grids, temporal support, named clock references, transformations between clocks, and provenance. Downstream libraries such as fmrimod own interpolation, HRF convolution, TR sampling, and design-matrix realization.

Architectural contract

- Each FeatureSeries, EventSeries, and TrackSeries declares the clock used by its temporal coordinates.
- Heterogeneous features remain on their native grids; no finest-common-grid construction or implicit resampling.
- Clock conversion is explicit and value-preserving: target_time = scale * source_time + offset_s.
- Temporal support is explicit: point, fixed window, or per-row interval, including onset/center/offset anchoring.
- Shared clock mappings live in a TemporalContext associated with a durable feature bundle.
- Cross-clock alignment requires an explicit mapping and never silently interpolates.
- Natural-features does not own HRF convolution or conversion to a TR design grid.

Deliverables

1. Temporal reference specification
- Add a normative docs specification covering ClockRef, ClockMap, SupportSpec, TemporalContext, native-grid preservation, mapping direction, validation, and non-goals.
- Use the canonical example where stimulus time 30 s equals scan-relative time 7 s.
- Explain stimulus, media, experiment, run, and scan clocks and positive/negative onset offsets.

2. Core temporal types
- Add typed clock references, invertible/composable affine clock maps, temporal support declarations, and a shared temporal context.
- Add invariant tests for composition, inversion, monotonicity, incompatible mappings, and coordinate conversion without value or row-count changes.

3. Feature-object and stimulus integration
- Strengthen FeatureSeries, EventSeries, TrackSeries, and stimulus wrappers so timing coordinates have explicit references and support.
- Preserve compatibility with existing times_s, onset_s, offset_s, start_offset_s, and legacy TimebaseSpec values through a documented migration path.
- Propagate temporal declarations through every public extractor and derived feature.

4. Bundle and alignment model
- Introduce a durable heterogeneous feature bundle, or evolve ExtractFeaturesResult, to carry features plus their shared TemporalContext.
- Make Timeline and FeatureAlignment clock-aware.
- Replace feature_t0_s semantics with an explicit ClockMap while providing compatibility wrappers where required.
- Use declared support or per-row bounds instead of inferred median-spacing intervals.

5. Lossless storage and catalog identity
- Round-trip schema, timebase, clock reference, temporal support, mappings, and provenance through NPZ, Zarr, Parquet, catalog records, and manifests.
- Correct existing NPZ omissions and restore custom schema values in readers.
- Include a temporal-context digest in artifact identity so identical values on different clocks cannot collide.
- Add exact round-trip tests for every temporal object and supported format.

6. Interchange and downstream handoff
- Export native values, timestamps or bounds, declared clock, requested target-clock coordinates, and provenance without accepting a TR or HRF.
- Keep long-form interchange natural for heterogeneous grids.
- Reject ambiguous cross-clock wide merges unless an explicit mapping and join policy are supplied.
- Define a dependency-free payload that fmrimod can adapt to its event, continuous-feature, sampling-frame, and regressor APIs.

7. Scope and compatibility cleanup
- Document HRF, lagging, design concatenation, TR resampling, and direct fmrimod adapters as legacy or experimental conveniences rather than the core architecture.
- Move consumer-specific adapters downstream when practical.
- Apply the public API compatibility policy, schema-version migration notes, changelog entries, and deprecation window for any stable API changes.

8. Documentation
- Update the feature-object schema, feature-object concepts, stimulus timing, timeline alignment, table interchange, fMRI windowing/query pages, API reference, public API policy, release process, and cookbook examples.
- Add a dedicated cookbook showing multiple native sampling rates and conversion from stimulus time to scan time without resampling.
- Clearly state the library boundary: natural-features owns temporal bookkeeping; modeling libraries own rendering and convolution.

Acceptance fixture

- One scan run spans 0-200 s with TR 1.77 s recorded as downstream acquisition context.
- Stimulus time 30 s maps exactly to scan-relative time 7 s.
- Feature A is sampled every 100 ms, feature B every 500 ms, and feature C uses irregular event intervals.
- All remain on their native grids before and after serialization.
- All can be expressed on the scan clock without changing values or row counts.
- NPZ, Zarr, and Parquet round trips preserve the full temporal contract.
- A downstream fmrimod integration consumes the payload without invoking natural-features HRF or TR-resampling functions.
- Documentation builds and examples execute as part of the release gate.
set.title: Establish lossless temporal clocks and native-grid feature contracts
